method: Scatterplot matrix with linked brushing. Each scatterplot shows pairwise relationships between soil nutrients and crop yield. Districts encoded through color. Interaction includes zooming and tooltip details.
objective: Identify key nutrient-yield correlations to prioritize soil enhancement strategies

method: Vertical grouped bar chart per district. X-axis: Soil types, grouped bars show frequency. Secondary axis line chart overlays average yield. Color encoding distinguishes soil prevalence vs yield metrics.
objective: Compare soil type distribution with agricultural productivity across districts

method: Box plots arranged horizontally. X-axis: Soil type categories. Y-axis: Organic Matter (%) ranges. Whiskers show min/max, boxes show quartiles. Interactive hover reveals district distribution details.
objective: Analyze organic matter variability across soil types to identify amendment priorities

method: Parallel coordinates plot showing interactions between pH, nitrogen, and yield. Districts encoded as polylines. Brushing filters clusters of similar soil-yield profiles.
objective: Discover combinations of pH/nitrogen levels associated with high yield clusters

method: Cascading filter system. Primary filter: Season (Kharif/Rabi). Updates linked bubble charts showing soil type vs yield size-encoded by production. Color indicates district.
objective: Assess seasonal variations in soil-yield relationships for crop rotation planning

method: Bidirectional bar chart. Left bars show districts with highest phosphorus levels. Right bars show districts with highest yields. Connecting lines highlight mismatches for targeted interventions.
objective: Identify districts needing phosphorus optimization despite high yields
